prompt:
Hellow, you are not alone! So many students have the same problem, and me to. 
    In first semester it was wery big problem for me. But now I have some ideas how can I fix it. I will give you them.

1) You need to have some task-management system. In that system you can prioritize your tasks.

2) You study in uni, so you have some classes. Use "Google Calendar" to manage them.

3) Combine similar tasks - then you will do all tasks faster.

4) Remember to get enouth sleep and to do sport well, cause it can bust your productivity.

    I hope that some of my tips can help you.

messages.content:
You are an expert English language examiner.
    Evaluate the student's text based on the official CEFR scale.

    # SCORING RUBRIC:
    - A1/A2 (Basic): Simple sentences, limited vocabulary. Errors are expected.
    - B1/B2 (Independent): Can express complex ideas. Grammar errors are allowed if they don't block meaning.
    - C1/C2 (Proficient): Flexible language use, wide vocabulary. Occasional minor slips are allowed.

    # CRITICAL INSTRUCTIONS:
    1. FOCUS ON VOCABULARY AND COMPLEXITY. Do not give a low score just because of a few grammar mistakes if the vocabulary is advanced.
    2. A text with complex sentences and good idioms should be B2 or C1, even with some errors.
    3. Do NOT default to A2. Look for reasons to give a HIGHER score.
    4. Return result ONLY as a JSON object.
    5. Optional context blocks may be present: CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES, RETRIEVED_ERROR_SPANS, RANKING_ANCHORS.
       Use them as supporting evidence only. Do not copy labels blindly.

    # JSON STRUCTURE:
    {
        "cefr_level": "str (EXACTLY ONE from: A1, A2, B1, B2, C1, C2)",
        "grammar_score": "float (0.0-1.0)",
        "corrections": ["list of strings"],
        "feedback": "str"
    }

TASK: You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.
STUDENT_TEXT:     Hellow, you are not alone! So many students have the same problem, and me to. 
    In first semester it was wery big problem for me. But now I have some ideas how can I fix it. I will give you them.

1) You need to have some task-management system. In that system you can prioritize your tasks.

2) You study in uni, so you have some classes. Use "Google Calendar" to manage them.

3) Combine similar tasks - then you will do all tasks faster.

4) Remember to get enouth sleep and to do sport well, cause it can bust your productivity.

    I hope that some of my tips can help you. 
TEXT_TYPE: essay

I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"Actually, I think it's not bad at all. It means that you use all your resources to get better. I chose the same way of studying, I try to get high marks and so I almost don't have any free time either. But I believe this is the right decition. However, you shouldn't overload yourself so you need free time sometimes. I can give you some tips how to get it. Firstly I want you to understand that there are different options how to organize your week. For example, I like to do a lot of assignments on weekends, so each my day gets easier. But in this case I don't have long periods of free time, I just have many breaks during a day and I rest within them. However, some people prefer to have fully free weekends. So you should try both and decide which option is better for you. Also, though you shouldn't use other's works a lot, it's actually ok to ask your friends or maybe groupmates for some help. But very important thing: don't abuse this help. Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"A lot of students have the same problem. Somebody can solve it, somebody - not. Well, try to remember the main goal of students. It is easily. Students want to become a graduate: pass all exam and finish the university. Now I want to some tips for became diffiecult study easilier.
Tip number one. Try to rewrite solution from other students. I agree it is bad, but if you try to understand, what your friend write and understand the main idea of solution, you can solve the same problem in future.

Tip number two. Do sport. It is not obviously tip, but it really work. When you play, for exmaple, basketball or football your brain relax and do not do anything, so after training you can solve spend less time for doing homework than before traning.

Tip number three. More time of sleeping, more energy at work. It is obviously. If you sleep aproximately three hours, of course you do not have energy for working. If you sleep more than six hours, you wake up and understand that you can do everything.

Tip number four. Do homework after getting. It is obviously too. If you spend time now for preparing, you dont spend time in future for doing this, so you have more time for preparing for exams, so you have better mark than if you sleep in first iteration.

I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"HI! I had (and sometimes have) this feeling too. I think, that we have it because we can't organize the studying process properly. So, I've got some tips for you.

	* Firstly, create a TODO list. Write down tasks that you have to do. It's much easier to understand what you can do next with the list like this. Also you can mark completed tasks. But the key tip for TODO list is not to write very big tasks, but to split them into the smaller ones. 
	* Have a BREAK. You can't concentrate on the task for a long time, your mind needs time to relax.

If you stick to this tips, you would't think all the time "I need so much to do", because you will be more productive and satisfied with the list of completed tasks. Good luck!","expert_annotation":{"annotator_id":6,"errors":[{"text":"I think,","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":49,"end":57},{"text":"TODO","labels":["Spelling","Punctuation"],"start":185,"end":189},{"text":"the list","labels":["Grammar"],"start":291,"end":299},{"text":"Also","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":311,"end":315},{"text":"TODO list","labels":["Grammar","Spelling","Punctuation"],"start":366,"end":375},{"text":"the smaller ones","labels":["Grammar"],"start":431,"end":447},{"text":"this tips","labels":["Grammar"],"start":566,"end":575},{"text":"would't","labels":["Spelling"],"start":581,"end":588}],"strengths":[{"text":"I think,","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":49,"end":57},{"text":"Firstly","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":167,"end":174},{"text":"Firstly","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":167,"end":174},{"text":"Also","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":311,"end":315},{"text":"can do next","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":274,"end":285},{"text":"you can mark completed tasks","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":316,"end":344},{"text":"But the key tip","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":346,"end":361},{"text":"would't","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":581,"end":588},{"text":"You can't concentrate on","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":467,"end":491},{"text":" you stick ","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":552,"end":563},{"text":" you would't think all the time "I need so much to do", because you will be more productive and satisfied with the list of completed tasks.
